questions.question: Let A and B be two subsets of R. Prove that if A and B are separated, then A ∪ B is disconnected. (5 marks)
questions.hint: A separated set means that there exist disjoint open sets U and V such that U ∩ A ≠ ∅ and V ∩ B ≠ ∅.

questions.question: Consider the function f: R → R defined by f(x) = 0 if x is irrational, and f(x) = 1 if x is rational. Prove that this function is continuous on R. Explain why this is the case. (8 marks)
questions.hint: Note: This function is well-defined and continuous. Consider the definition of continuity and the nature of the function.

questions.question: Let A be a connected subset of R. If A is bounded, prove that A is compact. (7 marks)
questions.hint: Note: To prove compactness, consider the definition of compactness and the fact that A is connected and bounded.
